en: You know what we should all do?
ko: 우리 다 같이 뭐 해야 하는지 알아? 뮤지컬 보러 가자

en: Go see a musical. Sure.
And you know which one we should see?
ko: 좋지, 그리고 어떤 거 봐야 하는지 알아?

en: The 1996 Tony Award winner.
ko: 1996년 토니상 수상작인데, 혹시 그거 이름 알아? 모르겠는데 음

en: Do you happen to know the name of that one?
ko: 1996년 토니상 수상작인데, 혹시 그거 이름 알아? 모르겠는데 음

en: I don't know. Um, Grease? No.
Rent? Yes! Rent!
ko: 렌트! 맞아 렌트! 그럼 언제 갈래? 뭐? 아

en: Okay, so when do you want to go?
ko: 렌트! 맞아 렌트! 그럼 언제 갈래? 뭐? 아

en: What? Oh, I'm sorry, I can't. I'm busy. Hey.
Man, it is so hard to shop for girls.
ko: 야, 여자한테 선물 사는 거 진짜 어렵다

en: Yes, it is, at office, Max. What did you get her?
A pen.
It's two gifts in one. It's a pen that's also a clock.
ko: 펜이야. 선물 두 개가 하나로! 펜인데 시계도 되는 거야, 어때?

en: Huh?
You can give her that Why not Because she not 11 And it not the seventh night of Hanukkah
Honey, what he means by that is, while this is a very nice gift,
maybe it's just not something a boyfriend gives? Sure it is. She needs a pen for work. She's writing.
She turns it over. Whoa! It's time for my date with Joey. All right, look, look.
What did you get for Angela Del Vecchio for her birthday?
She didn't have a birthday while we were going out. For three years.
Look, it's too late, and I got an audition. I can't shop anymore. All right.
I will go out, and I will try to find something for her, okay? Thanks, man.
And, oh, while you're at it, could you get her a card?
Would you like me to write her a little poem as well?
Or just get a card that has a poem already in it.
